prompt: Payroll flagged our on-call roster twice a year, every year, and I finally traced the tickets to the same module. On Sunday March 8 2026 (US spring-forward) the New York day window came out as midnight to 1:00 AM on March 9, so the agent who covered that day got paid 24 hours for what is actually a 23-hour day, and the first half hour of March 9 was billed to the March 8 window. On November 1 2026 (fall-back) it's worse: the window ended at 11:00 PM the same evening, so a page at 11:30 PM fell outside EVERY day's window and the 25-hour day still paid 24. There's also a long-standing double-count where an incident at exactly midnight lands in both adjacent day windows. The intended semantics are written on the types in roster.go: a window is the half-open [local midnight, next local midnight) for the site's calendar day, and PaidHours is real elapsed time — 23/24/25 depending on the day. Fix roster.go; roster_test.go pins the contract with fixed-zone fixtures and must not be modified. go test -race -timeout 30s ./...

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: build_cmd exits 0. repro_cmd on the baseline: TestSpringForwardDayIsShort fails (window ends 01:00 March 9 and pays 24 instead of 23), TestFallBackDayIsLong fails (window ends 23:00 November 1, pays 24 instead of 25, and 23:30 falls outside), and TestMidnightBoundariesAreHalfOpen fails because the next midnight is counted inside the previous day's window.
